title: Period
short: The time for one full cycle of an oscillation, T = 1/f, measured in seconds (s).
full:
The period T is the time taken for one complete cycle — one full swing of a pendulum, one full wave to pass. It is the reciprocal of frequency, T = 1/f: a fast oscillation (high frequency) has a short period.

A pendulum that ticks once per second has a period of 1 s and a frequency of 1 Hz.

The SI unit is the second (s).
